message:
feat(init): add --auto project-pack detection for dcg init

Add --auto, --dry-run, and --project-dir flags to `dcg init` that scan
the working directory for project files and automatically enable the
relevant rule packs. Detection covers:

- Containers: Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, Containerfile (Podman)
- Infrastructure: Terraform (.tf), Pulumi, Ansible
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, CircleCI
- Kubernetes: kubectl dirs, Helm charts, Kustomize
- Cloud: AWS (serverless/SAM), GCP (Cloud Build), Azure
- Databases: driver keywords in package.json, Cargo.toml, requirements.txt, etc.
- Package managers, secrets managers (Vault, 1Password)

--dry-run previews what --auto would enable without writing config.
--project-dir allows scanning a directory other than the cwd.

Also adds a "Project pack detection" check to `dcg doctor` that warns
when project files suggest packs that are not currently enabled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
